title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward shines with 10 hits and 12 stolen bases in 12 spring training games for the Red Sox.
Max Ferguson impresses with positional versatility and leads all Red Sox in RBIs despite likely playing mostly in minors.
Tayron Guerrero performs well in relief, with six strikeouts, offering potential value to Boston's bullpen.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson ranks second in 5-on-5 goals for the Bruins this season and remains with the team post-trade deadline.
Bruins coach Marco Sturm expressed confidence in keeping Arvidsson, valuing his scoring and veteran presence on the team.
Arvidsson's expiring contract and on-ice contributions make him a potential short-term asset for the Bruins' future plans.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots trade center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears, prompting a reshuffle of their offensive line.
Jared Wilson may move to center, with Patriots seeking a new left guard this offseason.
Potential additions include free agents Isaac Seumalo, David Edwards, and draft prospect Emmanuel Pregnon.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry, 48, from Stockton, California, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Make-A-Wish children in Massachusetts-Rhode Island.
For 15 years, Perry has been inspired by the courage and hope of Wish children, motivating his marathon efforts.
Perry honors both surviving and deceased Wish children, focusing on raising funds and awareness through his marathon run.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots predicted to sign Seahawks edge rusher Boye Mafe to a three-year, $48 million deal with $25 million guaranteed.
Boye Mafe recorded 44 pressures in 2025 and was a crucial part of the Seahawks' defensive line in their Super Bowl run.
ESPN's Dan Graziano predicts Colts receiver Alec Pierce will sign with the Chargers, not the Patriots, for $80 million over three years.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at Boston's House of Blues as part of his 'Under the Powerlines Tour' on March 6, 2026.
Welles' setlist featured 13 original songs and several covers, including Black Sabbath's 'Paranoid' and John Denver's 'Take Me Home, Country Roads'.
The Arkansas native combined sharp political commentary with a mix of country and rock, engaging the audience with his satire and energetic performance.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run and had three RBIs in Japan's 8-6 win over Korea at the World Baseball Classic.
Yoshida's performance contributed to Japan's 2-0 record in pool play, adding to his strong start with 4-for-7 hitting.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game in Japan, sporting a Dodgers uniform, while accompanying his wife on a trip.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, 25, is running her first Boston Marathon for Team Eye and Ear to support Mass. Eye and Ear.
Aboujaoude-Quirk, a Roslindale native, has been a patient at Mass. Eye and Ear for nearly 10 years.
Mass. Eye and Ear offers specialized research and the only 24-hour emergency department for ENT trauma in Massachusetts.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler joined ESPN/ABC's top NBA broadcast team in August 2025, alongside Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson.
Legler became ESPN/ABC's top NBA analyst in 2025 after being with the network since 2000.
Tim Legler is set to call NBA Finals with the top broadcast team this season after previous analyst shifts.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to NBA action after 298 days with 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists in the Celtics' win.
Maine native Cooper Flagg debuted at TD Garden, scoring 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists for the Mavericks.
Flagg's debut drew many fans from Maine, celebrating his NBA journey and connection with mentor Jayson Tatum.
